subsystems.rationale: This is the highest-level authority surface for what the final machine is supposed to be.
subsystems.summary: Final-system architecture that frames the whole project as a distributed cognition machine rather than a single model.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when the final-system shape, first principles, or top-level cognitive strata change.

subsystems.rationale: Exact truth should live in exact systems whenever possible.
subsystems.summary: Exact-truth doctrine for the Cathedral.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when deterministic truth boundaries or exact-store responsibilities change.

subsystems.rationale: The Cathedral needs a canonical structure for the map-first substrate that binds memory, routing, contradiction, lineage, and recompilation into one executable doctrine.
subsystems.summary: Persistent cognitive-topology substrate for the Cathedral.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when the substrate doctrine, layer map, or cross-layer invariants change.

subsystems.rationale: The substrate only becomes self-compiling if runtime exhaust is turned into better route priors, summaries, and durable structure.
subsystems.summary: Background compilation, exhaust reuse, compression, and self-rewrite loops.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when compiler jobs, compression families, promotion logic, or consolidation loops change.

subsystems.rationale: The map is not one graph; it is a stack of typed graph surfaces with attached trust and contradiction state.
subsystems.summary: Typed graph stack, confidence lattice, and contradiction/refutation surfaces.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when graph families, edge semantics, confidence vectors, or contradiction handling changes.

subsystems.rationale: FCS needs a first-class authoring and lowering surface that preserves substrate semantics while lowering only regular dense work into fast kernels.
subsystems.summary: Language and compiler surface for expressing and lowering FCS semantics.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when DSL constructs, IR layers, legality rules, scheduling doctrine, or backend-lowering boundaries change.

subsystems.rationale: The substrate only works if different memory planes have explicit roles, representations, and transfer rules.
subsystems.summary: Cold, warm, hot, and speculative memory-plane structure for the substrate.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when memory planes, staging rules, or object-granularity policies change.

subsystems.rationale: The substrate needs a formal interface for what models do, what they do not own, and how model-adjacent artifacts enter the map.
subsystems.summary: Deterministic/probabilistic split, translators, and parameter-system interface to dense models.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when translator rules, model roles, or parameter-artifact boundaries change.

subsystems.rationale: The substrate needs one reusable shell law so all later layers compose instead of drifting into incompatible object families.
subsystems.summary: Universal artifact, node, payload, and contract law for the substrate.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when node fields, artifact classes, universal verbs, or identity semantics change.

subsystems.rationale: The substrate needs an explicit lowering from durable structure into compact live execution state.
subsystems.summary: Hot route sketch, hydration pipeline, and live execution loop for the substrate.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when route-sketch fields, hydration behavior, or turn-loop arbitration changes.

subsystems.rationale: The system needs multiple graph layers because one graph is not enough.
subsystems.summary: Layered graph hierarchy for the Cathedral.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when graph layers, graph boundaries, or graph-to-memory relationships change.

subsystems.rationale: The machine learns at different speeds in different places.
subsystems.summary: Multi-speed learning and consolidation loops.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when fast, medium, or slow learning loops change.

subsystems.rationale: The Cathedral depends on memory being split into species rather than collapsed into one store.
subsystems.summary: Memory-species decomposition for the Cathedral.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when memory species, boundaries, or cross-memory responsibilities change.

subsystems.rationale: The system is a civilization of specialized minds, not one emperor model.
subsystems.summary: Model-species society for the Cathedral.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when model species, synthesis layers, or model-society roles change.

subsystems.rationale: The system must move through staged retrieval, shaping, synthesis, and critique rather than one-pass guessing.
subsystems.summary: Canonical staged query path for the Cathedral.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when the end-to-end query stages or their boundaries change.

subsystems.rationale: The Coding Foundry is the bounded subsystem used to build and operationalize the coding species of the larger system.
subsystems.summary: Cathedral-compatible coding lane that builds and later lives inside the larger system.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when the coding lane’s purpose, scope, or top-level boundaries change.

subsystems.rationale: The coding lane must not ask models to hallucinate exact repo facts that can be stored exactly.
subsystems.summary: Exact coding-fact domains and boundaries for the Foundry.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when deterministic coding domains, keys, or exact-fact ownership changes.

subsystems.rationale: The Foundry needs its own memory mapping while staying Cathedral-compatible.
subsystems.summary: Coding-lane mapping of memory species.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when coding-memory responsibilities or boundaries change.

subsystems.rationale: The coding lane itself is a model society, not one model plus prompts.
subsystems.summary: Scouts, judges, packet builders, route controllers, synthesis models, critics, and consolidators for the coding lane.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when coding-lane species or role assignments change.

subsystems.rationale: The coding lane must move through typed packets rather than prompt sludge.
subsystems.summary: Canonical packet family and packet-boundary discipline for coding work.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when packet families, packet invariants, or packet lineage rules change.

subsystems.rationale: The coding lane must emit parameter lineage that can later attach to the Cathedral parameter graph.
subsystems.summary: Lineage metadata for checkpoints and runtime artifacts used by the coding lane.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when artifact ancestry, validity, or compatibility metadata changes.

subsystems.rationale: Successful repeated behavior must have a path to become durable structure.
subsystems.summary: Promotion and consolidation hooks across fast, medium, and slow loops in the coding lane.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when promotion rules, consolidation triggers, or loop boundaries change.

subsystems.rationale: The lane needs bounded hot state for what is currently being worked.
subsystems.summary: Active run state for one coding attempt or route.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when active run state, ambiguity handling, or route-state shape changes.

subsystems.rationale: The harness is the operational spine that runs the coding lane safely and economically.
subsystems.summary: Rust control-plane harness for the coding lane.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when the harness service set, boundaries, or control-plane assumptions change.

subsystems.rationale: Artifacts need durable, content-addressable custody separate from workflow logic.
subsystems.summary: Durable custody layer for artifacts and retrieval handles.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when artifact persistence, manifests, or retrieval surfaces change.

subsystems.rationale: The broker isolates backend-specific semantics from the rest of the harness.
subsystems.summary: Backend invocation mediation boundary for model-serving calls.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when backend mediation, invocation envelopes, or large-payload rules change.

subsystems.rationale: The gateway owns client-facing admission and should stay separate from scheduling and execution.
subsystems.summary: Ingress and egress boundary for the harness.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when ingress contracts, admission checks, or outward API shaping changes.

subsystems.rationale: Not all model tiers should stay loaded the same way all the time.
subsystems.summary: Hot, warm, and cold runtime-plane distinctions for serving economics.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when plane definitions or loading economics change.

subsystems.rationale: The harness needs a clean boundary for code execution and tool runs.
subsystems.summary: Isolated process and workspace execution boundary.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when sandbox execution rules, isolation, or workspace lifecycle changes.

subsystems.rationale: The scheduler owns work classification, queue placement, and runtime-plane decisions.
subsystems.summary: Placement and lifecycle decision boundary for the harness.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when scheduling authority, leases, retries, or plane selection changes.

subsystems.rationale: Observability must exist without becoming the hidden workflow authority.
subsystems.summary: Observability boundary for logs, traces, and metrics.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when telemetry surfaces, trace shape, or operational visibility changes.

subsystems.rationale: The training system preserves separate role training and its cost-aware hardware logic.
subsystems.summary: Role-specific model and hardware training plan for the coding lane.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when role training, datasets, or hardware policy changes.

subsystems.rationale: Role-specific training only works if the dataset boundaries are explicit.
subsystems.summary: Dataset directions and role-specific training data mappings.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when dataset mappings, corpus directions, or role-data relationships change.

subsystems.rationale: Quality must be preserved while still exploiting favorable compute economics.
subsystems.summary: Cheapest-acceptable-hardware policy and escalation triggers.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when hardware classes, escalation triggers, or economic assumptions change.

subsystems.rationale: Each role keeps its own training lane and checkpoint family.
subsystems.summary: Per-role model family and role separation rule.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when role count, role identity, or base model assignment changes.

subsystems.rationale: Training and serving economics are linked through which models stay loaded and when.
subsystems.summary: Training-linked hot, warm, and cold runtime economics.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when training-driven runtime-plane assumptions change.

subsystems.rationale: The system must be buildable visually at depth, and the visual system plus bound subdocumentation must be able to change truth.
subsystems.summary: Truth-bearing visual authoring and compilation system.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when the visual authoring system, truth flow, or projection strategy changes.

subsystems.rationale: The machine must be drawable and navigable at depth.
subsystems.summary: Schematic or harness authoring surface for topology construction.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when authoring-surface assumptions or topology authoring rules change.

subsystems.rationale: Truth must be changeable through the visual system, not only through downstream artifacts.
subsystems.summary: Truth-bearing documentation attached to visual structures.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when the fields or rules of truth-bearing subdocumentation change.

subsystems.rationale: The GUI is another compiled view of the same canonical structural model.
subsystems.summary: Operator, observability, and self-visibility GUI projections.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when GUI/control-plane projection boundaries or view families change.

subsystems.rationale: Regeneration becomes a fistfight if compiler-owned and human-owned zones are not separated.
subsystems.summary: Owned-zone rule for generated structure versus custom behavior.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when package ownership rules or regeneration boundaries change.

subsystems.rationale: One authored structural model must project outward into many useful surfaces.
subsystems.summary: Downstream projections to code, docs, inventories, and wiki surfaces.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when generated outputs or projection boundaries change.

subsystems.rationale: The visual system only gains leverage if its authored truth can be compiled and regenerated systematically.
subsystems.summary: Compiler passes from authored truth into IR and projections.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when compiler stages, extraction rules, or validation passes change.

subsystems.rationale: The system needs a machine-stable truth layer for regeneration and downstream targets.
subsystems.summary: Machine-canonical structural layer compiled from authored truth.
subsystems.touch_when: Touch this when structural IR fields, boundaries, or authority rules change.
